title: soldanella × richteri subsp. marmarossiensis
intro: Soldanella × richteri subsp. marmarossiensis, also known as Marmaro soldanella, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the Primulaceae family, specifically a cross between Soldanella montaguiana and Soldanella alpina. This plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Marmaro soldanella include its potential to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, colds, and flu, as well as its ability to act as an expectorant and a mucilaginous agent. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various respiratory issues.
